preFixBehavior: Accepted base64-encoded Java serialized payload and deserialized with ObjectInputStream.readObject().
postFixBehavior: Accepts only application/json and parses it safely with Jackson into a Map, preventing gadget-chain deserialization.
evidence: src/main/java/com/owasp/lab/controller/InsecureDeserializationController.java: method uses ObjectMapper.readValue(body, Map.class) and consumes application/json

preFixBehavior: Accepted raw username and password concatenated into native SQL and returned user data including cleartext password.
postFixBehavior: Uses parameterized SQL and password hashing; returns only id, username, and role.
evidence: src/main/java/com/owasp/lab/service/UserService.java: createNativeQuery("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = :username", User.class).setParameter("username", username)

preFixBehavior: Passed the username query directly into a raw native SQL string, allowing boolean injection.
postFixBehavior: Uses a parameterized native query that treats the search term as a literal value.
evidence: src/main/java/com/owasp/lab/service/UserService.java: createNativeQuery("SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = :username", User.class).setParameter("username", username)

preFixBehavior: Seeded and registered users stored plaintext passwords in the database.
postFixBehavior: Seed and register flows encode passwords with DelegatingPasswordEncoder (BCrypt) before persistence.

vulnerabilityTitle: Broken access control for user enumeration
preFixBehavior: Allowed any anonymous user to enumerate all user records.
evidence: src/main/java/com/owasp/lab/controller/UserController.java: listUsers() checks caller authorities and throws AccessDeniedException if not ROLE_ADMIN
src/main/java/com/owasp/lab/config/SecurityConfig.java: authorizeHttpRequests lists public matchers and requires authenticated() for others

preFixBehavior: Returned any user profile for any id without ownership or admin checks.
postFixBehavior: Allows only the authenticated owner or an ADMIN to read the profile.
evidence: src/main/java/com/owasp/lab/controller/UserController.java: getProfile() verifies caller is authenticated and either ADMIN or username matches target.getUsername()

preFixBehavior: Allowed anyone to transfer funds from any account without authenticating as the source owner.
postFixBehavior: Requires authentication and verifies the caller owns the from-account or has ADMIN privileges.
evidence: src/main/java/com/owasp/lab/controller/AuthController.java: transfer() checks caller != null and verifies caller.getUsername().equals(from.getUsername()) unless ADMIN

preFixBehavior: Returned user-controlled name directly into HTML without escaping.
postFixBehavior: Escapes the name query parameter before rendering it into the HTML response.

preFixBehavior: Rendered database comment text into HTML without escaping.
postFixBehavior: Escapes author and body fields before concatenating them into the HTML page.
evidence: src/main/java/com/owasp/lab/controller/CommentViewController.java: HtmlUtils.htmlEscape(c.getAuthor()) and htmlEscape(c.getBody()) used when rendering

preFixBehavior: Returned the user's plain-text password in the authenticated login response.
postFixBehavior: Returns only id, username, and role; the password field is omitted.

preFixBehavior: Hardcoded secrets present in application.properties and rendered into pages.
postFixBehavior: Secrets sourced from environment variables via placeholders; SecretConfig exposes beans that return env-sourced values.

vulnerabilityTitle: Missing CSRF Protection on State-Changing Endpoints
preFixBehavior: CSRF protection was disabled across the app.
postFixBehavior: CSRF protection re-enabled; explicit ignoring only for H2 console and a few JSON endpoints where appropriate.

preFixBehavior: Register accepted a client-supplied role allowing creation of ADMIN accounts.
postFixBehavior: Register forces role to USER server-side and encodes passwords before save.
evidence: src/main/java/com/owasp/lab/controller/AuthController.java: register() forces role to "USER" and calls passwordEncoder.encode(password)

vulnerabilityTitle: JWT Signing Key With Insufficient Entropy
preFixBehavior: Hardcoded JWT signing key in source control.
postFixBehavior: JWT signing key is sourced from environment variable placeholder; no hardcoded default provided.

vulnerabilityTitle: Verbose SQL Logging with Sensitive Data Exposure
preFixBehavior: Hibernate SQL and bind parameters were logged at DEBUG/TRACE, exposing sensitive data.
postFixBehavior: SQL logging disabled and parameter tracing turned off; log levels set to WARN/OFF.

vulnerabilityTitle: No Security Logging / Monitoring on AuthN Events
preFixBehavior: Silent exception swallowing and no structured logs for failed login or query errors.
postFixBehavior: Failed logins and query errors emit structured SLF4J WARN logs for monitoring.
evidence: src/main/java/com/owasp/lab/service/UserService.java: catch blocks log warnings for failed queries

preFixBehavior: No CSP, HSTS, or secure response headers; frameOptions disabled.
postFixBehavior: Content-Security-Policy, HSTS, Referrer-Policy and frameOptions SAMEORIGIN configured.

vulnerabilityTitle: H2 Console Exposed on Production-Equivalent Port
preFixBehavior: H2 console enabled by default and exposed on the web UI.
postFixBehavior: H2 console disabled by default; can be enabled via H2_CONSOLE_ENABLED=true in env.

preFixBehavior: Rendered API keys and DB passwords into HTML responses via @Value autowired fields.
postFixBehavior: No secret values rendered; page indicates secrets are redacted and sourced from env vars.
evidence: src/main/java/com/owasp/lab/controller/VulnerabilityController.java: response contains 'Secrets: redacted - sourced from environment variables' and no @Value interpolation
